empires.legalNote: Aprilice AB, 70% owned by Elektroskandia Sverige AB (itself wholly owned by Sonepar, rebranding to 'Sonepar Sverige'). Tracked as a single entity under the Aprilice brand per DSEG's call.
KP Sverige AB, owned by OKQ8 Solar Holding AB (part of OKQ8).
empires.revenueTrend: declining sharply (from 849M FY24 to 394M FY25; headcount 101->67)

empires.legalNote: Swedish subsidiary of Krannich Solar GmbH & Co. KG (Germany, family-owned).
empires.statusNote: Marked exited/quit Swedish market per DSEG's direct read of the market. Note: registry data (allabolag.se) showed FY2024 revenue ~370M SEK, +42% YoY, which conflicts with this call -- flagged for awareness, DSEG's read takes precedence.

empires.legalNote: Senergia AB, part of Senergia Nordic AB. Independent, minority stake held by Investment AB Oresund.
empires.revenueTrend: volatile (1.53B in 2023 after 97% growth, then -55% to 691M in 2024)

empires.legalNote: Energy Group AES AB, independent/founder-led, HQ Forshaga (Varmland) with a Stockholm sales office.
empires.revenueTrend: growing, battery-storage led (registry figures inconsistent with company's own growth narrative -- flagged, small player either way)

empires.legalNote: Ahlsell Sverige AB, CVC Capital Partners-owned. Absorbed Solelgrossisten (solar/EV/battery specialist wholesaler) fully into its electrical division as of Nov 2024 -- Solelgrossisten no longer tracked as a separate brand.
empires.statusNote: Currently low/passive activity in the solar category per DSEG's read, despite owning the former Solelgrossisten business.
empires.revenueTrend: group revenue declining (-3.5%); solar/EV/battery sub-segment not separately reported
empires.confidence: high on structure, low on solar-specific performance

empires.legalNote: Rexel Sverige AB, subsidiary of Rexel S.A. (France, Euronext Paris). Exited direct ownership of EV-charging infrastructure (transferred to Wattif EV Sweden, 2024-2025) but still sells EV charging hardware.
Parkys Solar AB, family-owned (Boershult Holding AB / Wahlberg family). NOTE: unrelated to 'Parkys Solcellsinstallationer AB' (bankrupt Jan 2026, different parent -- do not conflate).
empires.revenueTrend: recovering from a 73% collapse in 2024, still unprofitable

empires.legalNote: Part of the Danish-listed Solar Group. Newly added to DSEG's competitor list.
empires.revenueTrend: growing, actively expanding into solar/battery/EV (new Kumla logistics center, 2026)

empires.legalNote: Was an installer/EPC (not a distributor) -- included historically per DSEG's original list, tracked here as a fallen entity.
empires.statusNote: Declared bankrupt 17 March 2026 after a failed reconstruction; inventory and customer list absorbed by Svea Solar.

disintermediation_threats.description: Not tracked as empires competing for settlements. Modeled as a board-wide adverse effect (like Catan's robber) that erodes addressable market share for ALL distributor players, including DSEG -- rather than a defeatable competitor.

members.legalNote: Installer/EPC roll-up, PE-backed (Altor). Imports/installs in-house, ~50,000 residential customers.
German-owned installer roll-up (acquired Cellsolar). Buys battery cells direct from BYD at group scale; opening its Heartbeat AI platform to third-party installers.
